content:
Hi all, I'm working on migrating kconfirm (Rust) to use the upstream kconfig parser (C) so I need to add Rust bindings for this.

I'm pretty sure these do not belong in rust/kernel/ since the kconfig interpreter isn't actually part of the kernel, but rather host tooling.

My best guess is that these bindings belong in scripts/kconfig/ alongside the C code, am I on the right track?
Did you already speak with the relevant upstrema maintainers of kconfig parsing?
The kconfig parser is partially unmaintained (only maintained in odd-fixes status), but I can ask them

I'm pretty sure these do not belong in rust/kernel/ since the kconfig interpreter isn't actually part of the kernel, but rather host tooling.

Yes, it should probably go into the Makefile of kconfirm itself. Later we can generalize if needed.
If you mean having manual bindings because it is simple enough and stable, then I would just put the .rs in your own folder, yeah.
Alternatively, if the maintainers think they could be useful for others in the future, they could go into their folder.

Just to confirm, if I have manual bindings, then I would put them in scripts/kconfig/kconfirm/

(with kconfirm/ being "my directory")?

But if the maintainers of kconfig are OK with maintaining the bindings for everyone, which would be cleaner/nicer (but more work for them), then in their folder and you could include! or use path = or similar, right?

Well, I'm planning to move kconfirm under scripts/kconfig to address one of the issues raised by the maintainer from RFC v3, which was that having scripts/kconfirm and scripts/kconfig would break tab auto-complete, a complaint from Linus
In that case, I meant wherever you plan to put kconfirm :)

And if everything is actually going to be inside the Kconfig folder (or a subfolder, I mean), then it may make sense to have the bindings at that level, rather than your own folder, but it wouldn't change much I assume.
